title: Always Your Flavorite
description: Hero flavor bursts from the bottle, revealing ingredients. The lineup stretches behind it — there's always more. Ran nationally across print, outdoor, and digital.

caseStudy.extendedDescription: Burnett's needed a campaign that could spotlight any single flavor while reinforcing the range behind it. I designed a system where the hero flavor bursts from the bottle, revealing the ingredients in a way that makes them look alive and appetizing. The rest of the lineup stretches out behind it, bleeding off the edge — there's always more. One layout, infinite flavor swaps. The campaign ran nationally across print, outdoor, and digital.

title: Ellis Park Billboard Concepts
description: Conceptual billboard campaign for Ellis Park Racing. Each version riffs on real racehorse names.  The visual is tied to the tag line beautifully, the horse name coming out of the mouth mid-yell,  "Go Horse!" works both ways: you're cheering for your pick and losing your voice doing it.

title: Hpnotiq Sparkle
description: Ad campaign for Hpnotiq's Sparkle line and Endless Summer seasonal promotions. Bold color palette and nightlife energy.

title: Logo Design Collection
description: Brand identity work across multiple industries — from healthcare to consumer products to startups. Each mark designed to communicate clearly at any scale.

title: Endless PAMAbilities
description: Dozens of cocktails composed into mosaic art around the bottle. Scaled across seasons — Valentine's, Holiday. Ran nationally in print, outdoor, and digital.

caseStudy.extendedDescription: The client wanted to communicate the vast mixability of PAMA. I took a show-don't-tell approach — dozens of unique cocktails, each professionally mixed and shot, composed into a single mosaic art piece around the bottle. The full composition lives inside a branded frame built on PAMA's pomegranate motif. The concept scaled across seasons — Valentine's Day, Holiday — same framework, fresh executions. Ran nationally in print, outdoor, and digital. Award-winning campaign.
caseStudy.processNotes: Award-winning campaign that ran nationally in print, outdoor, and digital.

title: Rittenhouse Rye
description: Complete packaging overhaul for a heritage whiskey brand dating back to 1934. Currently on shelves nationwide.

caseStudy.extendedDescription: A complete packaging overhaul for a heritage whiskey brand dating back to 1934, named after Philadelphia's Rittenhouse Square. The new design draws from the original label language while making it feel current and authoritative — honoring the history without being stuck in it. Currently on shelves nationwide.

title: SiteX Corp Website
description: Designed and co-developed a website for SiteX Corp, a professional clothing company. The site lets users swap the background pattern between three animal prints — a direct tie-in with their "Dress for Your Role" campaign.

caseStudy.extendedDescription: SiteX needed a web presence that reinforced their campaign message: dress like you mean it. I designed the full site and co-programmed it with a teammate. The standout feature is an interactive background switcher — three animal print patterns (zebra, peacock, leopard) that the user can toggle on the fly. It's a small detail that turns the site into an extension of the campaign, not just a brochure next to it.

title: EPIC Brand Campaign
description: Four-part brand values campaign — Engaging, Protective, Innovative, Committed. Each ad pairs bold typography with product photography to communicate Berry's core identity across trade publications.

caseStudy.extendedDescription: Berry needed a unified campaign that could communicate its brand pillars across trade publications and internal communications. I designed a system where each value — Engaging, Protective, Innovative, Committed — gets its own ad with distinct imagery but a consistent layout framework. The campaign reinforced Berry's position as a Fortune 500 packaging leader.

title: Pack Expo Trade Show Booths
description: Large-scale booth design and environmental graphics for Pack Expo — the packaging industry's biggest trade show. Designed Berry's presence across 2018, 2019, and 2022 shows.

caseStudy.extendedDescription: Pack Expo is where the packaging industry does business. I designed Berry's booth presence across multiple years — from initial concepts through production-ready files. Each year required a fresh approach while maintaining brand consistency. The 2022 booth won a Silver ADDY Award.
caseStudy.processNotes: Silver ADDY Award winner (2022). Concepted, designed, and produced booth graphics across three consecutive show years.

title: Packaging Design
description: At Berry Global, I designed a wide variety of packaging solutions, including custom sample kits, demonstration mock products that highlighted the company's capabilities, and production packaging for real retail products sold nationally.

captions.packaging_delizioso.jpg: Mockup product demonstrating Berry's in-mold labeling capabilities. The brand name is printed see-through, revealing the ice cream inside — every facing on the shelf shows a slightly different swirl of product through the text. A small detail that creates real shelf differentiation.
captions.packaging_can.jpg: Sample kit designed for the cannabis packaging market after multi-state legalization opened the door. Contains child-resistant packaging solutions, but the design leans into the new industry — cool, clean, green-themed. A fun nod to an emerging category without losing the professionalism.
captions.packaging_jarcollection.jpg: Sample kit built on a tight budget. Found a supplier that came in under cost, then designed the kit to punch above its weight — the result still feels premium despite the constraints.
captions.packaging_resinite.jpg: Packaging for Berry's Resinite stretch film brand. The design hides the cross-section of a film roll inside the box art, creating a striking shelf presence that still reads as industrial and trustworthy.

title: Protect What's Inside
description: Trade ad for Berry's barrier packaging line. An oyster guards its pearl underwater — the visual metaphor sells the product before you read a word. Ran in packaging industry trade publications.

title: Verdant Goodbye
description: Sustainability-focused brand ad for Berry Global. The concept reframes packaging waste as a solvable problem — Berry's products designed with end-of-life in mind.
